results.title: REST list endpoints return non-deterministic results across identical calls — even with `orderBy` on a sortable field and a `gte` filter
results.summary: Min Jaccard across 10 pairs = 0.000 (1.0 means identical sets). Identical calls — same skip/limit, working orderBy on id, working `gte` filter on createdDate — returned different ID sets.

results.title: `orderBy` is honored on `id` but ignored on `createdDate` and `updatedDate`
results.summary: orderBy={"id":"asc"} produced 3/49 alphabetic inversions on UUIDs (effectively sorted). Same syntax on createdDate: ≥21/49 inversions across 8 encodings. On updatedDate: ≥22/49 inversions across 8 encodings. Neither date field is orderable. collate=true on createdDate: 26 inversions.

results.title: `limit` parameter silently clamped at 100
results.summary: Requested limit=500, received 100 rows (clamped to 100). No 4xx, no warning header.

results.title: Cursor pagination on `id` (the recommended workaround for skip+limit) is also non-deterministic
results.summary: 3 runs of cursor pagination (orderBy={"id":"asc"}, no skip, page 2+ uses where={id:{gt:lastIdOfPrevPage}}) returned mostly-disjoint ID sets across runs. Min Jaccard = 0.096 across 3 pairs. Per-run page sizes also differed despite identical queries: run1=100/100/100, run2=100/54, run3=100/100/61.

results.title: Cursor pagination on `id` returns duplicate IDs across pages within a single run
results.summary: Across 3 independent cursor paginations of 4 pages each, 3/3 runs produced duplicate IDs across pages — 224 duplicate ids in total. 0 returned ids violated the cursor invariant outright (id ≤ the cursor passed in to that page's where filter). With orderBy={"id":"asc"} and where={id:{gt:lastId}}, no id should appear in more than one page of the same run.

results.title: Even MAX-cursor pagination (strictly correct cursor pagination) returns mostly-disjoint subsets of a larger universe across runs
results.summary: 3 runs of MAX-cursor pagination (orderBy={"id":"asc"}, page N+1 uses where={id:{gt: client-computed MAX of page N}}, createdDate>=2026-04-15T00:00:00Z). Each run produced 0 within-run duplicates (as expected by construction). But the runs disagreed on coverage: union=175 ids, intersection=40, smallest run=104, largest run=110. The smallest run missed 71 of the 175 ids that exist (40.6%). Min cross-run Jaccard = 0.373. Page sizes per run: run1=[100,7], run2=[100,10], run3=[100,4] — pagination exits early (page 2 often returns 0–few records) even though the universe is larger.
